ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-18-2023 Granted Per Written Order

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Aug-29-2023 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jul-25-2023 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-20-2023 Continued To Jul-25-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-11-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Emily Nashban, via Zoom, for and with Timothy Ryan, Administrator.

The court queries counsel regarding the filing of a stipulation in the related civil matter. Counsel advises that she has been advised to file the stipulation closer to the trial date in the civil matter. The court offers its tentative ruling to grant preliminary distribution and order a reserve to cover potential costs regarding the civil matter until that matter is resolved; allow seventy-five percent of statutory fees requested; deny the request for extraordinary fees without prejudice. Ms. Nashban requests that the court allow a reserve slightly more than $100k to cover potential costs related to the civil matter. The court and counsel discuss status of a close in this matter. The court advises counsel that it intends to add a status hearing on close of estate to the proposed order.

The matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-06-2022 Continued To Sep-20-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-28-2022 Continued To Sep-06-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Emily Nashban appeared by zoom for and with Timothy Ryan by zoom.
Marcus McElhenney (attorney in the civil matter) appeared by zoom.

The court stated that Ms. Nashban's declaration is not particularly persuasive, that it is hearsay, and questions why it did not come directly from trial counsel. Mr. McElhenney stated that he is here to answer any questions the court may have. The court noted that the declaration says that no punitive would come from the estate, that no facts are alleged which sounds like a motion to strike, but it hasn't seen one filed in the civil action, questions who would pay for the damages in causes of action 1, 2 and 3, and stated that it wants to have all of this in writing so that there is a record of it. Mr. McElhenney stated that they have a million dollar policy, that on the face of it there is $62,000.00 to $65,000.00 in damages, that even if they lost and $65,000.00 in damages was awarded against the estate, he believes the damages will be picked up by the insurance company, and that he believes they will be released from the civil matter in the next two weeks. Mr. McElhenney also stated that the complaint was poorly worded, that it doesn't apply to the estate, that it's to the insurance company and that the punitive damages are not properly pleaded. The court stated that it needs a basis to determine an appropriate amount of a reserve, that it needs reliable evidence upon which it can find that this is a reasonable reserve under these circumstances, and noted that there is no Lis pendens here. Ms. Nashban stated that the property was sold months ago and that there is no request for special notice from the plaintiff. The Petition for Final Distribution to Approve Non-Pro Rata Distribution, etc. is continued to September 6, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Counsel is to make every effort to get something filed as soon as possible and deliver courtesy copies to the Probate Department.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Xiao. Not reported

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-17-2022 Continued To Jun-28-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Emily Nashban, via Zoom, for Timothy Ryan, Petitioner.

The court and counsel discuss the status of the civil litigation and the request to reserve $100,000. The court advises that as the petition is currently framed, there is no basis for the court to grant the reserve request and offers counsel the options to either continue the matter to allow her to submit additional evidence or to take the petition off calendar and set a hearing date for status. Counsel requests a continuance to allow for the filing of additional evidence.

The matter is continued to Jun-28-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Gomez. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
